summarization.a: The figure shows the intensity of C 1s binding energy peaks at various energies
summarization.b: The figure displays the intensity of Li 1s binding energy peaks at various energies
summarization.c: The figure illustrates the intensity of O 1s binding energy peaks at various energies

a.question: Was depth profiling by sputtering procedure used to investigate the composition?
How are the C1s XPS peaks fitted?
a.answer:
The binding energy scale was calibrated by using the hydrocarbon contamination C 1s peak at 285.0 eV. As expected, the C 1s spectrum shows a main peak at 290.1 eV assigned to the carbonate environment in Li2CO3. The other peaks fitted with a red line are associated with
adventitious carbon on the sample surface

c.question: How is the slight asymmetry in O 1s peak accounted for?
c.answer:
The O 1s spectrum consists of a peak with a maximum at 531.8 eV. The shape of the peak is slightly asymmetric. To visualize the asymmetry a second peak is fitted to the data. The asymmetry can be explained by the crystallographic structure of Li2CO3 in which two oxygen atoms have an identical environment,
but the third oxygen has a different environment of surrounding atoms

b.question: What is the stoichimetry of the film?
b.answer:
The obtained stoichiometry for this flm was: 
1. 33.0 at% lithium
2.  17.7 at% carbon 
3. 49.3 at% oxygen
corresponding to stoichiometric Li2CO3
